author: @Mr.Unacceptable
text: Is there a way to use your conductive inks as a resistor? My thought is if you can why not coat thread with the ink and then create a sweater or jacket with it. Run a battery source through it it and the entire thing warms up to body temperature. No need for coils or pads or washing problems. i can't clean my heated sweater because it would kill all the coils. With your ink as the resistor baked into the thread you don't need to worry about washing ruining it. If one thread fails the rest will compensate.

author: @matthewellisor5835
text:
I know that we humans are inherently flawed and, like the editor says, "if it bleeds, it leads" but this is a wonderful reminder that most people, most of the time  will be kind and generous to others.

I have significant training and work history in a security-related field so it's hard sometimes to Not be actively looking for threats when we're out. But if I pause to reasonably consider what would happen to my young children if we were walking along and I was hit by a bus or had a MI or was otherwise suddenly incapacitated, I assess that a kind, generous and caring person would overwhelmingly likely find a way to help them.

Long ramble to say: Thanks for one more pillar to bolster the position that most people mostly do good!

author: @jaminoes_
text: It is easy to be kind when you have money. This sounds extremely cynical, but it sadly extremely real.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I am sorry mate it isn't cynical it is just wrong. Kindness springs from the heart not the wallet.

replies.author: @prevengeix8551
replies.text: I would agree that it is extremely cynical. At no point was anything said about Cristof having money. maybe he already has a 3d printer he won somewhere else. maybe he's just like me and knows that even if he got one that he doesn't have the patience for the programing required to use it. maybe he just thought someone else might get more enjoyment. perhaps he is just a naturally giving person. Or it could be EXACTLY as you describe. I prefer a more positive outlook and belief in the goodness of my fellow humans including you.

replies.author: @schlega2
replies.text: Have you seen rich people? Kindness seems to be rarer and rarer as you go up the income scale.

replies.author: @jaminoes_
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkeringGood point. Perhaps I should have said: realizing acts of kindness gets easier with money. You are right, kindness exists regardless of wealth. I stand corrected.

replies.author: @jaminoes_
replies.text: @prevengeix8551 wait, I was not your fellow human?
